title: tax — trait composition
description: Three owned traits. `BelongsToTaxJurisdiction` is the FK trait for downstream models (TaxRate, TaxExemption). `HasTaxRates` / `HasTaxCalculations` are inverse-side conveniences on TaxJurisdiction (for HasTaxRates) and Tenant (for HasTaxCalculations), exposing scoped query helpers. Order matters when composed with BelongsToTenant — tenant MUST come first so the scope chain observes the tenant context.

owned.description: Mixed into any domain model that carries a `tax_jurisdiction_id` (or `issuing_jurisdiction_id`) FK. Adds the `jurisdiction()` belongsTo relation + a `resolveInJurisdiction(?TaxJurisdiction)` query scope. Used by TaxRate + TaxExemption. Refuses to save a row whose jurisdiction points at a jurisdiction in a different tenant than the row itself (unless the jurisdiction is platform-reference, tenant_id IS NULL — that's always allowed).
usedBy: tax::TaxRate (required tax_jurisdiction_id — every rate anchored to exactly one jurisdiction)
tax::TaxExemption (required issuing_jurisdiction_id — the jurisdiction that ISSUED the certificate)
composition_notes: Order: `BelongsToTenant` must appear BEFORE `BelongsToTaxJurisdiction` in the trait list so the tenant scope resolves first.
Refuses cross-tenant jurisdiction references (except platform-reference jurisdictions with tenant_id IS NULL).

owned.description: Composed onto the TaxJurisdiction model itself. Adds `->rates()` (hasMany over TaxRate), `->activeRates()` (scope filter effective_from <= today AND (effective_to IS NULL OR effective_to > today)), `->ratesFor(TaxRateType, TaxRateAppliesTo, ?CarbonImmutable)` (returns the single applicable rate for the tuple).

Composed onto the Tenant model (imported from tenancy module). Adds `->taxCalculations()` (hasMany over TaxCalculation), `->recentTaxCalculations(int $days = 30)`, `->annualTaxSummary(int $year)` (returns per-jurisdiction rollup). Kept small — the tenant model is heavy already; this trait exposes only the convenience read methods the finance module needs.

framework.reason: TaxRate, TaxJurisdiction, TaxExemption. NOT TaxCalculation (immutable — no soft-delete + no hard-delete; migrates to archive at 7 years).

foundation.reason: TaxJurisdiction (searchable by name, country_code, state_code) + TaxExemption (searchable by certificate_number - redacted, customer_id, exemption_type). NOT TaxRate + TaxCalculation (accessed via jurisdiction / invoice_line relations).

tenants.reason: Every owned row carries tenant_id per tenancy-columns.md §3. NOTE: TaxJurisdiction uses a modified variant that allows tenant_id IS NULL for platform-reference rows.

audit.reason: Every write to tax_rates / tax_jurisdictions / tax_exemptions / tax_calculations is compliance-relevant (financial retention 7 years, 10 for Enterprise).

activity.reason: TaxExemption + tax_provider_configs — verification workflow decisions feed the staff-visible activity feed. NOT TaxRate + TaxJurisdiction + TaxCalculation — either high-volume or already covered by the audit trail.

tax_rate.notes: Order matters: BelongsToTenant precedes every scope-observing trait. HasActivityLog omitted — rate changes are high-volume + covered by audit.

tax_jurisdiction.notes: Uses BelongsToTenantOptional (module-specific variant of BelongsToTenant) because tenant_id is NULLABLE for platform-reference jurisdictions. Platform-reference rows bypass the tenant scope entirely; tenant-scoped rows behave normally.

tax_exemption.notes: SoftDeletes for the archive path (soft-delete + 7-year grace before hard-purge). HasActivityLog critical — the verification workflow (verify / reject) is a staff decision worth surfacing in the activity feed.

tax_calculation.notes: NO SoftDeletes — calculations are IMMUTABLE. They migrate to archive at 7 years (10 for Enterprise) via PurgeExpiredCalculationsJob. NO HasActivityLog — high-volume + already covered by audit. NO Searchable — calculations are accessed via invoice_line + calculated_at + tenant filters, not free-text search.
